other_observers: I am the finder; 2 other birders Jonelle and Bruce were there and photographed the gull once I pointed it out. They posted a link to photos on COBIRDS, including flight shots.
description: A tiny larus, not much larger than the many Black Terns at the lake. It had a dark brown mantle and crown witha dark eye spot behind the eye on a white-ish face. The gull was white beneath with a white tail and dark terminal band. A black 'M' pattern was formed on th back of the wings when the bird was in flight. See photo.Behaviors: Flying around remaining water of drained lake, occasionally resting on the mud.Call: nonePlumage: juvenile
